Description: The Part detail view page provides a detailed view of a single part. It is divided into sections covering Part Details (IPN/Name/Description/Revision/Keywords/External Link/Units) and tabs for Stock/Allocations/BOM/Build Orders/Used In/Suppliers/Purchase Orders/Sales Orders/Stock History/Test Templates/Related Parts/Parameters/Attachments/Notes.
Entity/Objective: Part Detail Display
Preconditions: A part exists in the system with a defined name, description, category, IPN, keywords, and units. User is logged in.
Action: Navigate to the Parts section, click on a specific part name in the category list to open the Part detail view. Click the 'Show Part Details' toggle button.
Expected Behavior: All part details are displayed correctly: IPN
column15: and Units fields are all visible and populated with accurate data.

Description: The Part detail view page provides a detailed view of a single part. It is divided into sections covering Part Details (IPN/Name/Description/Revision/Keywords/External Link/Units) and tabs for Stock/Allocations/BOM/Build Orders/Used In/Suppliers/Purchase Orders/Sales Orders/Stock History/Test Templates/Related Parts/Parameters/Attachments/Notes.
Entity/Objective: Category Breadcrumb Navigation
Preconditions: A part exists assigned to a nested category (e.g., Electronics > Resistors). User is logged in.
Action: Navigate to a part detail page and observe the category breadcrumb list displayed in the top navigation bar. Click one of the breadcrumb category links.
Expected Behavior: The breadcrumb list shows the correct hierarchical category path. Clicking a category link navigates the user to that category's part list page.

Description: The Part detail view page provides a detailed view of a single part. It is divided into sections covering Part Details (IPN/Name/Description/Revision/Keywords/External Link/Units) and tabs for Stock/Allocations/BOM/Build Orders/Used In/Suppliers/Purchase Orders/Sales Orders/Stock History/Test Templates/Related Parts/Parameters/Attachments/Notes.
Entity/Objective: Part Tabs Visibility Based on Attributes
Preconditions: A part exists that is marked as Assembly, Component, Purchaseable, Salable, and Testable. User is logged in.
Action: Navigate to the Part detail page and observe the available tabs. Verify that the following tabs are visible: Variants (if Template), Stock, Allocations (if Component or Salable), Bill of Materials (if Assembly), Build Orders, Used In (if Component), Suppliers (if Purchaseable), Purchase Orders (if Purchaseable), Sales Orders, Test Templates (if Testable), Related Parts, Parameters, Attachments, and Notes.
Expected Behavior: All expected tabs are displayed based on part attributes. Tabs that are conditional (Suppliers/Purchase Orders only for Purchaseable parts; Allocations for Component/Salable) appear only when the related attribute is enabled.

Description: The Part detail view page provides a detailed view of a single part. It is divided into sections covering Part Details (IPN/Name/Description/Revision/Keywords/External Link/Units) and tabs for Stock/Allocations/BOM/Build Orders/Used In/Suppliers/Purchase Orders/Sales Orders/Stock History/Test Templates/Related Parts/Parameters/Attachments/Notes.
Entity/Objective: Invalid Part URL Access
Preconditions: The user is logged in. No part exists with ID 99999.
Action: Manually enter an invalid part ID in the browser URL (e.g., /part/99999/).
Expected Behavior: The application displays a meaningful error message such as 'Part not found' or returns a 404 error page. The application does not crash or show a blank page.

Description: The Part detail view page provides a detailed view of a single part. It is divided into sections covering Part Details (IPN/Name/Description/Revision/Keywords/External Link/Units) and tabs for Stock/Allocations/BOM/Build Orders/Used In/Suppliers/Purchase Orders/Sales Orders/Stock History/Test Templates/Related Parts/Parameters/Attachments/Notes.
Entity/Objective: Stock Tab Export Function
Preconditions: A part exists with at least 3 stock items in different locations. User is logged in.
Action: Navigate to the Part detail page, click the 'Stock' tab, then click the 'Export' button to download the stocktake data.
Expected Behavior: A dialog opens showing export options. Upon selecting options and confirming
column9: a file is downloaded containing data for all stock items for the selected part. The exported data accurately matches the stock items displayed on the Stock tab.

Description: Parts can have multiple defined parameters. Parameters are key-value pairs linked to a part and can be defined by parameter templates. Parameters on locked parts cannot be created/edited/deleted.
Entity/Objective: View Part Parameters
Preconditions: A part exists with multiple parameters defined (e.g., voltage: 5V, resistance: 100 Ohm). User is logged in.
Action: Navigate to the Part detail page, click the 'Parameters' tab.
Expected Behavior: All parameters associated with the part are displayed in a table
column9: showing each parameter name and its value accurately.

Description: Parts can have multiple defined parameters. Parameters are key-value pairs linked to a part and can be defined by parameter templates. Parameters on locked parts cannot be created/edited/deleted.
Entity/Objective: Add a New Parameter to a Part
Preconditions: An unlocked part exists. User is logged in with edit permissions.
Action: Navigate to the Part detail page, click the 'Parameters' tab. Click the 'Add Parameter' button. Fill in the parameter name (selecting from available templates) and a value, then submit the form.
Expected Behavior: The new parameter is successfully added to the part and displayed in the parameters table with the correct name and value.

Description: Parts can have multiple defined parameters. Parameters are key-value pairs linked to a part and can be defined by parameter templates. Parameters on locked parts cannot be created/edited/deleted.
Entity/Objective: Edit an Existing Parameter
Preconditions: An unlocked part exists with at least one parameter. User is logged in with edit permissions.
Action: Navigate to the Part detail page, click the 'Parameters' tab. Click the edit button for an existing parameter. Change the parameter value and submit.
Expected Behavior: The parameter value is updated in the database and the updated value is immediately reflected in the parameters table.

Description: Parts can have multiple defined parameters. Parameters are key-value pairs linked to a part and can be defined by parameter templates. Parameters on locked parts cannot be created/edited/deleted.
Entity/Objective: Attempt to Edit Parameters on a Locked Part
Preconditions: A locked part exists with defined parameters. User is logged in.
Action: Navigate to the Part detail page of a locked part, click the 'Parameters' tab. Attempt to click 'Add Parameter' or edit/delete any existing parameter.
Expected Behavior: The system prevents modification of parameters. Edit/delete/add buttons are either hidden or disabled for locked parts. If buttons exist and are clicked
column9: an error message displays: 'Cannot modify parameters of a locked part.'

Description: Parts can have multiple defined parameters. Parameters are key-value pairs linked to a part and can be defined by parameter templates. Parameters on locked parts cannot be created/edited/deleted.
Entity/Objective: Delete a Parameter from a Part
Preconditions: An unlocked part exists with multiple parameters. User is logged in with delete permissions.
Action: Navigate to the Part detail page, click the 'Parameters' tab. Click the delete (trash) icon for one of the parameters. Confirm the deletion in the confirmation dialog.
Expected Behavior: The parameter is removed from the part. The parameters table is refreshed and no longer displays the deleted parameter. A success confirmation may appear briefly.

Description: A Template part is one that can have variants created beneath it. Variants share serial number uniqueness and stock reporting is aggregated. Templates cannot have revisions (only variant parts can have revisions). Variants are created from the Variants tab using the 'New Variant' button.
Entity/Objective: Enable Template Attribute on a Part
Preconditions: A non-template part exists. User is logged in with edit permissions.
Action: Navigate to the part detail page of an existing part. Click the 'Details' tab. Locate the part options on the right-hand side. Toggle the 'Template' option to enabled (green/slider to the right).
Expected Behavior: The part is now designated as a Template part. The 'Variants' tab becomes visible on the part detail page. The Template toggle visually shows as enabled (green/right).

Description: A Template part is one that can have variants created beneath it. Variants share serial number uniqueness and stock reporting is aggregated. Templates cannot have revisions (only variant parts can have revisions). Variants are created from the Variants tab using the 'New Variant' button.
Entity/Objective: Create a Variant from a Template Part
Preconditions: A Template part exists. User is logged in with create permissions.
Action: Navigate to the Template part's detail page. Click on the 'Variants' tab. Click the 'New Variant' button. Fill out the 'Duplicate Part' form with a new part name and other details. Click 'Submit'.
Expected Behavior: A new variant part is created successfully. The user is redirected to the new variant part detail page. The new variant appears in the Variants tab of the template part. The variant is linked back to the Template part.

Description: A Template part is one that can have variants created beneath it. Variants share serial number uniqueness and stock reporting is aggregated. Templates cannot have revisions (only variant parts can have revisions). Variants are created from the Variants tab using the 'New Variant' button.
Entity/Objective: Verify Stock Aggregation Across Template and Variants
Preconditions: A Template part (e.g., Widget) with two variants (Widget-01 and Widget-02) exists. Each variant has stock: Widget-01 has 10 units, Widget-02 has 15 units. User is logged in.
Action: Navigate to the Template part (Widget) detail page. Click on the 'Stock' tab.
Expected Behavior: The Stock tab of the Template part shows an aggregated total of 25 units (10 + 15)
column9: representing the combined stock from all variants. Individual stock items for each variant are also displayed.

Description: A Template part is one that can have variants created beneath it. Variants share serial number uniqueness and stock reporting is aggregated. Templates cannot have revisions (only variant parts can have revisions). Variants are created from the Variants tab using the 'New Variant' button.
Entity/Objective: Attempt to Create a Revision of a Template Part
Preconditions: A Template part exists. User is logged in with create permissions.
Action: Navigate to the Template part's detail page. Open the 'Part Actions' menu (three vertical dots). Select 'Duplicate Part'. In the Duplicate Part form, attempt to fill in the 'Revision Of' field pointing to the Template part and provide a revision code. Submit the form.
Expected Behavior: The system rejects the operation and displays an error message indicating that template parts cannot have revisions. The new part is not created.

Description: A Template part is one that can have variants created beneath it. Variants share serial number uniqueness and stock reporting is aggregated. Templates cannot have revisions (only variant parts can have revisions). Variants are created from the Variants tab using the 'New Variant' button.
Entity/Objective: Verify Serial Number Uniqueness Across Variants
Preconditions: A Template part (e.g., Widget) has two variants: Widget-01 and Widget-02. Widget-01 has a stock item with serial number 'SN-001'. User is logged in.
Action: Attempt to create a new stock item for Widget-02 using the same serial number 'SN-001'.
Expected Behavior: The system rejects the operation and displays an error message such as 'This serial number is already in use by another variant.' The stock item is not created
column9: enforcing uniqueness of serial numbers across all variants of a template.

Description: Part Revisions allow tracking of design changes over time without affecting existing related data. A revision is itself a full part with its own stock/BOM/parameters. Key fields are Revision (code) and Revision Of (link to original part). Restrictions include no circular references
Entity/Objective: template parts cannot have revisions. Navigation between revisions via a dropdown selector.
Action: A non-template part exists (e.g., 'Circuit Board'). User is logged in with create permissions. Part Revisions are enabled in global settings.
Expected Behavior: Positive
column9: Navigate to the part detail page of 'Circuit Board'. Click the part actions menu (three vertical dots). Select 'Duplicate Part'. In the Duplicate Part form, set the 'Revision Of' field to 'Circuit Board' and set the 'Revision' field to 'B'. Submit the form.
column10: A new part revision is created successfully. The user is redirected to the new part revision page. The revision shows 'Revision: B' in its details and the 'Revision Of' field links back to the original 'Circuit Board' part.

Description: Part Revisions allow tracking of design changes over time without affecting existing related data. A revision is itself a full part with its own stock/BOM/parameters. Key fields are Revision (code) and Revision Of (link to original part). Restrictions include no circular references
Entity/Objective: template parts cannot have revisions. Navigation between revisions via a dropdown selector.
Scenario Type: Navigate Between Multiple Revisions Using Revision Selector
Action: A part with three revisions (Rev A, Rev B, Rev C) exists. User is logged in.
Expected Behavior: Positive
column9: Navigate to any one of the part revision pages (e.g., Rev A). Observe the 'Select Part Revision' dropdown at the top of the part page. Use the dropdown to switch to a different revision (e.g., Rev C).
column10: The revision selector dropdown is visible and lists all available revisions (A
column12: C). Selecting a different revision navigates the user to that revision's detail page. The page content updates to reflect the selected revision's data.

Description: Part Revisions allow tracking of design changes over time without affecting existing related data. A revision is itself a full part with its own stock/BOM/parameters. Key fields are Revision (code) and Revision Of (link to original part). Restrictions include no circular references
Entity/Objective: template parts cannot have revisions. Navigation between revisions via a dropdown selector.
Scenario Type: Verify Revision Selector is Hidden for Parts with Only One Revision
Action: A part exists with no revisions (only the original part, no duplicates with 'Revision Of' set). User is logged in.
Expected Behavior: Positive
column9: Navigate to the part detail page of the part that has no revisions.
column10: The 'Select Part Revision' dropdown selector is NOT visible on the page. The UI is clean and uncluttered with no redundant revision navigation element for a non-revisioned part.

Description: Part Revisions allow tracking of design changes over time without affecting existing related data. A revision is itself a full part with its own stock/BOM/parameters. Key fields are Revision (code) and Revision Of (link to original part). Restrictions include no circular references
Entity/Objective: template parts cannot have revisions. Navigation between revisions via a dropdown selector.
Scenario Type: Attempt to Create a Revision with a Duplicate Revision Code
Action: A part with revision 'A' already exists for 'Circuit Board'. User is logged in.
Expected Behavior: Negative
column9: Attempt to create a new revision for 'Circuit Board' with the same revision code 'A' (which already exists). Fill the Duplicate Part form with Revision Of = 'Circuit Board' and Revision = 'A'. Submit.
column10: The system rejects the operation and displays an error message such as 'A revision with this code already exists for this part.' The duplicate revision is not created.

Description: Part Revisions allow tracking of design changes over time without affecting existing related data. A revision is itself a full part with its own stock/BOM/parameters. Key fields are Revision (code) and Revision Of (link to original part). Restrictions include no circular references
Entity/Objective: template parts cannot have revisions. Navigation between revisions via a dropdown selector.
Scenario Type: Verify Original Part Data is Unaffected After Revision Creation
Action: An original part 'Circuit Board (Rev A)' with existing stock items and BOM is in the system. User has just created a new revision 'Circuit Board (Rev B)'.
Expected Behavior: Edge Case
column9: Navigate to the original 'Circuit Board (Rev A)' detail page and check its stock items, BOM, and parameters.
column12: and parameters for the original part (Rev A) remain unchanged and intact after the creation of the new revision (Rev B). No data is overwritten or lost.
